summary: Joel announces that his discussion forum has been running as an experiment with various hidden design decisions to improve discussion quality. He teases an upcoming essay explaining the forum's design choices but says it will only be distributed to email subscribers, not published on the web, due to 'Heisenberg effects' — the idea that explaining the moderation mechanics publicly would undermine their effectiveness.
key_points: Joel's discussion forum incorporates subtle, behind-the-scenes design decisions aimed at improving discussion quality
He references 'Heisenberg effects' — observing/explaining the system would change how people behave within it
The essay is deliberately kept off the public web to preserve the forum's dynamics
He uses exclusive content as an incentive to grow his email subscriber list
money_quotes: Although it may seem simple, there are a lot of subtle design decisions and magic-behind-the-scenes in hopes of improving the quality of discussion that takes place there.
Because it's full of Heisenberg effects, the essay won't appear on this web site, it will only go out via email to email subscribers.
key_insight: The best community moderation systems work precisely because their mechanisms are invisible to participants — explaining them publicly would undermine their effectiveness.
